summary: DHH celebrates the enduring brilliance of vi-style text editing, marveling that a tool created in 1976 remains fundamentally unchanged and deeply productive in 2024. He explains vi's core appeal through its grammar-based approach to text manipulation, where actions, scopes, and objects combine into powerful combos. Drawing an extended analogy to Street Fighter combo moves, he argues that vi transforms mundane text editing into a satisfying, game-like experience. He recommends Neovim with the LazyVim distribution as the best entry point for newcomers, and shares his own positive experience after switching from TextMate earlier in the year.
key_points: Vi's fundamental design of splitting insert mode from command mode has survived nearly 50 years essentially unchanged, defying tech's obsession with constant reinvention
Vi's real power is that it's a composable language for editing, built on an action-scope-object grammar that lets users construct unlimited combinations from a small set of primitives
The experience of mastering vi combos is game-like and dopamine-inducing, analogous to learning Street Fighter special moves
LazyVim provides an accessible entry point to Neovim, avoiding the daunting from-scratch configuration process
Despite the rapid pace of AI and modern tooling, some foundational tools persist because their core design was right from the start
money_quotes: For as much as some things in technology change, many other things stay the same. Like vi!
In 1976, most computers didn't even have a mouse. In 2024, most vi-successor users don't even need one when programming.
Vi makes manipulating text into a key-based form of Street Fighter.
It's not just an editor, it's a language for editing.
Once you learn the basic grammar of vi text manipulation, and you learn a few actions, scopes, and objects, you can string it all together in any combination possible.
It's just uniquely satisfying to string a handful of these combos together and see the text beneath you radically manipulated. In a way you just know would have been a drag to do in any other editor.
Neovim from scratch is like climbing Mount Everest without oxygen. If all you're interested in at first is a peek at the view, book that LazyVim helicopter tour before bothering the sherpas.
May vi reign for another fifty years and beyond!
key_insight: Vi endures because it's not merely a text editor but a composable language for text manipulation, whose action-scope-object grammar makes editing feel like mastering a fighting game's combo system.
